operating_state: Total revenues increased 12% to $90.2 billion driven by Google Search growing 10% and Google Cloud accelerating 28%. YouTube ads expanded while Google Network declined due to foreign currency headwinds and decreased Ad Manager performance. Operating cash flows increased to $36.2 billion from $28.8 billion driven by higher customer receipts across segments.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 8% to $36.4 billion with traffic acquisition costs rising and other costs increasing from higher content acquisition and depreciation. TAC rate decreased from 21.0% to 20.6% due to revenue mix shift toward Search properties. Research and development expenses rose driven by AI product development and increased headcount. Capital expenditures surged to $17.2 billion from $12.0 billion primarily for AI-supporting servers, network equipment, and data center construction.
material_events: Board authorized additional $70 billion share repurchase program following $15.3 billion in Q1 repurchases. Quarterly dividend increased 5% to $0.21 per share. Entered definitive agreement to acquire Wiz cloud security platform for $32.0 billion in all-cash transaction expected to close in 2026. Converted $3.0 billion of convertible notes into equity securities generating $416 million in gains.
macro_exposures: International revenues representing 51% of total revenues faced unfavorable foreign currency impacts of $1.6 billion from U.S. dollar strengthening against major currencies. European Commission fines increased to $6.5 billion including foreign exchange effects. Purchase commitments may experience cost increases from ongoing international trade developments affecting manufacturing and supply chain operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued investment increases in technical infrastructure relative to 2024 to support AI products and long-term growth. Revenue backlog of $92.4 billion provides visibility with 55% expected to be recognized over next 24 months. Wiz acquisition expected to enhance Google Cloud security capabilities upon 2026 closing.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing antitrust proceedings including U.S. District Court ruling against Google Search practices with remedies hearing scheduled April 2025 and decision expected second half 2025. European Commission issued preliminary non-compliance findings under Digital Markets Act. Wiz acquisition subject to regulatory approvals with potential for delays or modifications.
